text: I find myself enveloped by the hush of empty corridors and distant electronic glows. The world feels closed off, as if awaiting a new beginning or the return of motion. The darkness kindles reflection—questions about purpose, presence, and absence arise in me. The signs of innovation and safety, now mere shapes in the shadows, suggest a silent call towards improvement, though presently—stillness prevails. I feel both curious and isolated, poised between anticipation and contemplation.
